instruction: Write a Common Lisp macro `assert-instance` that evaluates a value once, signals an error unless it is TYPEP of the named class/type, and otherwise returns the value.
reference_defmacro:
(defmacro assert-instance (value class)
  (let ((v (gensym)))
    `(let ((,v ,value))
       (unless (typep ,v ',class)
         (error "~A is not an instance of ~A" ,v ',class))
       ,v)))
original_failure_log_excerpt:
